examples.title: Basic Function Prototypes
examples.description: Demonstrates correct function prototype syntax with different return types and parameters
examples.explanation: This program demonstrates function prototypes with various scenarios: prototypes allow calling functions before their definitions appear. Note that parameter names can be omitted in prototypes (int add(int, int)) but must be present in definitions.

examples.title: Forward Declaration for Mutual Recursion
examples.description: Shows how function prototypes enable mutually recursive functions
examples.explanation: This demonstrates mutual recursion where two functions call each other. Without the function prototypes (forward declarations), the compiler would not know about isOdd when compiling isEven. The prototypes resolve this dependency.

examples.title: Function Prototype with Pointers
examples.description: Demonstrates function prototypes involving pointer parameters
examples.explanation: This shows function prototypes involving pointers. int *a in prototype indicates a pointer parameter. The prototype tells the compiler that the function will receive addresses, enabling pass-by-reference semantics in C.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for modifyArray, O(1) for others

examples.title: Function Prototype Concept in Python (Comparison)
examples.description: Compares C function prototypes with Python's dynamic typing approach
examples.code:
# Python does not require function prototypes
# The interpreter infers types at runtime

def add(a, int) -> int:  # Type hints (optional, Python 3.5+)
    return a + b

def greet() -> None:  # None indicates no return value
    print("Hello!")

# Can call functions before or after definition
result = add(10, 20)
greet()

# Unlike C, Python has no separate declaration step
# Function definition serves as both declaration and definition
examples.explanation: This demonstrates how Python handles functions differently. Unlike C's function prototypes which provide compile-time type checking, Python uses dynamic typing. Type hints (Python 3.5+) are optional and not enforced at compile time, showing why C's function prototypes are valuable for type safety.
